sections.paragraph:
δήποτε adv. (Hom. et al.; pap, e.g., PLond III, 904, 22 [104 a.d.] p. 125; PFamTebt 15, 41; PTebt 381, 14; LXX) at any time; w. relative whatever J 5:4 v.l.; τί δ. just why (Maximus Tyr. 1, 5a; Lucian, Jupp. Conf. 16; Jos., Ant. 11, 89) Dg 1 (earlier style). —M-M.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Maximus Maximus Tyr , II a.d.—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 223
